content.full_text:
January 19, 1990

To: FPPC
Legal Division
Sacramento, CA 95814

Please help me determine which account the following items would,
if any, be taken from.

Account #1 would be a pre Prop 73 account, which has no money
raised in excess of the limitation.

Account #2 would be a campaign account established for the 1990
election.

1.     Providing travel benefits to a campaign or state employee.

2.     Providing travel benefits to the spouse of a campaign or state
employee traveling with the employee.

3.     Purchase of gifts for campaign employee.

4.     Purchase of meals while dining with constituents, legislative
matters discussed.

5.     Purchase of meals with campaign employee or volunteer,
campaign discussed.

6.     Purchase of meals with state employee, legislative matters
discussed.

7.     Bookkeeping supplies and materials, including but not limited
to; pencils, paper, folders, etc.

8.     Gas, oil and maintenance on vehicles for meetings with
volunteers, employees, and/or constituents on campaign related
matters.

9.     Christmas cards.   Mailing and printing.

10.    Photo sessions.   Photos may be used for campaign mailers.

11. Donations to civic groups. ie; boy scouts, cancer society, farm bureau raffles, etc.

12. Purchase of tickets to functions such as Chamber of Commerce dinner, Farm Bureau Crab Feed, Cattlemens Day, etc.

13. Purchase of tickets to functions such as Chamber of Commerce dinner, Farm Bureau Crab Feed, Cattlemens Day, etc. for employee or volunteer traveling on officials behalf.

14. Purchase of tickets to functions such as Chamber of Commerce dinner, Farm Bureau Crab Feed, Cattlemens Day, etc. for spouse of employee or volunteer traveling on officials behalf.

15. Photo equipment, supplies, computer hardware, supplies used for ongoing campaign and/or legislative purposes.

16. The purchase of year round, on-going public service announcements, or greeting card type announcements on the electronic media to maintain name identification.

17. On-going year round expenses for telephone, PG&E, water, janitorial, etc in office used for fundraising, and maintaining campaign records.

18. Insurance for office used for fundraising, and maintaining campaign records.

19. Security services for office used for fundraising, and maintaining campaign records.

20. Gifts for constituents. (retirement, birthday, fundraising inducements)

21. Dues to health club for employees.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

L.G. Statham
1008 10th St #514
Sacramento, Ca 95814
g

sections.question: Please help me determine which account the following items would, if any, be taken from. Account #1 would be a pre Prop 73 account, which has no money raised in excess of the limitation. Account #2 would be a campaign account established for the 1990 election. [Followed by 21 numbered items requesting account classification for various expenditures]
sections.question_synthetic: A candidate seeks guidance on whether 21 specified expenditure categories (including travel, meals, gifts, office supplies, equipment, and contributions) should be charged to a pre-Proposition 73 account or a 1990 campaign account.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No conclusion or FPPC response is present in this document. The document contains 21 numbered items requesting account classification guidance. Document appears complete with signature from requestor L.G. Statham.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from L.G. Statham to the FPPC asking for guidance on proper account classification for various campaign and legislative expenditures under Proposition 73, specifically whether items should be charged to a pre-Prop 73 account or a new 1990 campaign account.
